cataloging_subject.label: Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, Volume I
cataloging_subject.scope_note: Joseph Thomas Fowler's 1898 edited calendar and selection from five Durham Cathedral Priory office-account series, represented by the edition title page and printed pp. 1–284. It is not one medieval authored work, one manuscript, the complete surviving archive, or the complete three-volume Fowler publication. The historical account records, Fowler's editorial compilation, the printed volume, the repository encoding, and each physical roll or duplicate copy remain distinct layers.

catalog_summary.title: Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, Volume I
catalog_summary.author: Durham Cathedral Priory office-holders and accounting clerks
catalog_summary.date: Source records c. 1290–1535 under current cataloging; edited compilation first published 1898
catalog_summary.region: Durham Cathedral Priory and its estates in northern England

titles.value: Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, Volume I
Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, from the Original MSS., Vol. I
notes: Expanded capitalization from the 1898 title page; punctuation retained in normalized bibliographic form.

agents.name: Durham Cathedral Priory office-holders and accounting clerks
notes: A cataloging collective for the monks, deputies, and clerks responsible for creating the many separate accounts; not a claim of unified personal authorship.

agents.name: Joseph Thomas Fowler
notes: Editor, compiler, transcriber, and commentator of the 1898 volume.

scope.description: The individual Latin and vernacular account records calendared or excerpted by Fowler

notes: The schema role author is used broadly for documentary creation. Individual accountability belongs to named office-holders and clerks at item level, not to this collective as a literary author.

scope.description: The 1898 volume I text, headings, notes, arrangement, and calendar descriptions
Selection and ordering of five office series and their individual yearly accounts
Rolls and passages printed or extracted from the originals

notes: Fowler did not transcribe every record in full; brief mentions and abstracts are distinguished in lineage from printed or extracted text.

scope.description: English headings, physical descriptions, contextual remarks, footnotes, preface, and calendar notes

date_statements.display: c. 1290–1535 under current cataloging; Fowler's printed sequence is chiefly labelled 1303–1535

notes: The lower bound reflects the current catalogue's redating of the almoner rent-roll that Fowler mentioned on p. 199 as circa 1312. This is a source-record range, not one work-composition event.

First publication of Fowler's volume I edited compilation
Durham Cathedral Priory, its monastic household, church, offices, and central administration

language_statements.label: Middle English

scope.description: Vernacular words, phrases, annotations, headings, and occasional documentary passages embedded in Latin accounts

notes: The XML declares enm and lat at TEXT level; Middle English is not assumed to be the primary language of every item.

scope.description: Fowler's title, preface, headings, summaries, physical descriptions, notes, and connective prose

form_statements.description: A prose documentary calendar and selection comprising accounts, inventories, lists, tables, abstracts, headings, and editorial commentary; no verse structure is encoded.

scope.description: The yearly and shorter-period financial and administrative records in all five series
Status rolls, indentures, receipts, expenses, inventories, arrears, memoranda, and office administration
Provisioning, hospitality, clothing, buildings, land, livestock, rents, payments, and office expenses
Daily institutional, liturgical, charitable, medical, and social life of Durham's Benedictine priory

summaries.value: Fowler's first Durham account-roll volume calendars and selectively transcribes the surviving records of five monastic offices: the cellerer, responsible especially for provisioning and food; the hostiller, for guests and associated revenues; the chamberlain, for clothing and related household needs; the almoner, for charity, schools, estates, and dependent activities; and the master of the infirmary, for care, buildings, supplies, and property. The records preserve unusually detailed evidence for prices, diet, hospitality, labor, clothing, repair, landholding, charity, medicine, travel, material culture, and institutional life from the late thirteenth through early sixteenth centuries.

The encoded body has five edition-defined sections: Rotuli Celerariorum, 161 DIV2 items on printed pp. 1–112; Rotuli Hostillariorum, 186 items on pp. 113–164; Rotuli Camerariorum, 76 items on pp. 165–198; Rotuli Elemosinariorum, 147 items on pp. 199–258; and Rotuli Magistrorum Infirmarie, 83 items on pp. 259–284. The XML also includes the 1898 edition title page but omits the Society title pages, council resolutions, preface, and contents.

The 653 XML item containers represent Fowler's editorial sequence, not a physical-document inventory. The current catalogue has 862 entry groups explicitly cross-referenced to Surtees Society 99 and distinguishes duplicates, fragments, schedules, and corrected dates. Of 396 cross-referenced groups with public images, 509 distinct IIIF objects were cached separately: 463 complete explicit face/dorse inventories and 46 partial physical-object inventories. The remaining 466 cross-referenced catalogue groups expose no IIIF object and require further item-level crosswalking and access work.

extent.description: The repository XML contains 653 direct BODY/DIV1/DIV2 editorial item containers in five account-roll series: 161 cellerer, 186 hostiller, 76 chamberlain, 147 almoner, and 83 infirmarer. Two DIV3 elements label verso subdivisions within individual items. These are edition/encoding units, not a physical-roll count; the current catalogue's groupings and duplicate-copy distinctions produce different counts.

content_parts.label: Rotuli Celerariorum
content_parts.summary: Cellerers' accounts and related rolls, including receipts, kitchen and larder expenses, provisions, debts, staff, estates, and physical descriptions of surviving records.

content_parts.label: Rotuli Hostillariorum
content_parts.summary: Hostillers' accounts and status records concerning hospitality, guests, food and drink, dependent properties, church proctorships, and office expenditure.

content_parts.label: Rotuli Camerariorum
content_parts.summary: Chamberlains' accounts concerning clothing, cloth, footwear, bedding, household equipment, wages, repairs, and receipts.

content_parts.label: Rotuli Elemosinariorum
content_parts.summary: Almoners' rent-rolls, accounts, and status records covering charity, schools and children, estates, rents, buildings, provisions, clothing, and institutional obligations.

content_parts.label: Rotuli Magistrorum Infirmarie
content_parts.summary: Accounts of the masters of the infirmary concerning receipts, food, fuel, bedding, medicines, buildings, chapel and hall furnishings, staff, and dependent property.

evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD; IDG; FRONT; BODY/DIV1 and DIV2; PB milestones
evidence.summary: Supplies the title, identifiers, declared Latin and Middle English languages, title page, five office-series headings, item text and notes, pagination, and 2016 review history.

evidence.citation: Element and source-structure inspection of repository CME00046.xml.
evidence.locator: FRONT/DIV1; BODY/DIV1; DIV2; DIV3; P; NOTE; PB
evidence.summary: Counts one title-page DIV1, five body DIV1 series, 653 body DIV2 editorial items, two nested DIV3 verso units, 2,770 paragraphs, 107 notes, and 285 page milestones. The five body-series DIV2 counts are 161, 186, 76, 147, and 83.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00046 item record and full text.
evidence.summary: Confirms the public electronic item, title, inherited corporate author heading, 1898 publication, subjects, and public-domain assertion.

evidence.citation: Joseph Thomas Fowler, ed., Extracts from the Account Rolls of the Abbey of Durham, vol. I (1898).
evidence.summary: The complete edition scan confirms title, editor, date, publisher, five part titles and page ranges, documentary forms, languages, dates, places, and contents.

evidence.citation: Fowler, preface to Extracts from the Account Rolls, vol. I, pp. vii–viii.
evidence.summary: Explains Fowler's selection, transcription, and calendar aims and the relation of volume I to the three-volume project.

evidence.citation: Fowler, introduction to Extracts from the Account Rolls, vol. III, pp. i–lxvii (1901).
evidence.summary: Provides retrospective institutional, chronological, editorial, and subject context for the account-roll archive and the three-volume compilation.

evidence.citation: Durham University Library, Catalogue of Medieval Accounting Material in the Durham Cathedral Archive: office holders (obedientiaries).
evidence.summary: Supplies current series boundaries and extents, creator and holding context, exact physical objects and duplicate copies, re-datings, SS99 page cross-references, and access and publication conditions.

evidence.citation: Audit of 509 exact Durham IIIF objects linked from SS99-cross-referenced catalogue entries.
evidence.summary: Confirms the scale and physical distinctness of the public source surrogates, their face/dorse coverage, exact licences, and work-to-source mappings.

evidence.citation: Durham Cathedral Imaging Services and Durham University archives photography policy.
evidence.summary: Supports the request route and current limitation to images already on file; separate publication permission and onsite photography rules remain applicable.

open_questions.question: Which current physical account object or duplicate copy underlies each of the 653 Fowler/XML item containers?
next_steps: Create a many-to-many crosswalk keyed by part, Fowler heading, printed page, current catalog entry, shelfmark, duplicate letter, and IIIF ARK.
Keep Fowler's historical dates and the current catalogue's corrected dates as attributed assertions.

open_questions.question: What date range should discovery facets use when Fowler's earliest printed labels and the current catalogue's re-datings differ?
next_steps: Retain c. 1290–1535 as the current-catalog source-record envelope and do not rewrite individual Fowler headings until the item crosswalk is complete.
Add part- or item-level current dates only from exact catalog records.

open_questions.question: Can named accountants, deputies, clerks, and scribes be assigned consistently to each represented item without conflating accounting responsibility, authorship, and scribal production?
next_steps: Use current item headings and manuscript images to distinguish the accountable office-holder from the record's hand or clerk.
Keep the present collective creator display until item-level roles are verified.

notes: This record catalogs Fowler's edition-level selection because that is the coherent unit encoded by CME00046; medieval documentary creators are represented separately from Fowler's editorial responsibility.

The XML TEXT/@LANG value enm lat is respected but scoped: Latin is primary in the records, Middle English is embedded, and Fowler's substantial modern English editorial matter is added as a separate editorial language layer.
All 509 public SS99-linked Durham IIIF objects were cached separately. Of these, 463 explicitly expose all identified face/dorse units and 46 remain partial at the physical-object level.
The public manuscript images are not cleared for this project's commercial publishing workflow: exact manifests are CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 and Durham requires advance permission for published use.
The 2016 XML review date documents a later proofing and structural pass, not first publication or the medieval records' creation.
